da7c3639c0f99b64a14bf3d3aa123921.name:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all.

   of which cannot be easily swapped. We therefore stub `wsl?` via
   with-redefs where we need to simulate being inside WSL. > native-Linux (wsl? false) > returns ["127.0.0.1"] for any source-path

1c37c2791131497338919e3ea7bbecfb.name:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > :storage-state-path → setStorageStatePath > works for Paths with JSON-like substrings

4aa31e2c448c6b8ae33f967b6cc5a618.name:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > neither key > leaves both fields nil when neither key is present

   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > :storage-state → setStorageState (inline JSON) > handles JSON containing cookie paths (the bug that motivated this split)

9f73cbbd809213f565d18e021df0e065.name:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > both keys together > allows both keys simultaneously (Playwright keeps whichever was set last internally)

1eb65225ddd7890b56027adc464e322b.name:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > :storage-state-path → setStorageStatePath > writes only the path field (not the inline JSON field)

d8be29de0d526ef9a105041b0fcfab4c.name:
Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split.

   Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a
   fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic
   mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}`
   as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the
   `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix:
   use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > :storage-state → setStorageState (inline JSON) > writes only the inline JSON field (not the path field)
